hard.next_step: JRA 7/1: Reached out to Liz to get feedback on project that was mentioned at DPW NY
hard.ais_why:
• On the October 8 2025 demo, Erik Palkhiwala and Yana Levin raised LLM integration preferences directly. They asked Zycus to confirm that Merlin negotiation agents would work with Stemline's corporate Google Gemini instance rather than Azure or OpenAI. The Zycus team logged an action item to confirm whether all Merlin agents support alternative corporate LLMs and report back. That is buyer-driven AI architecture.
• Stakeholders pulled for AI-driven workflow automation to compress contract development, review and approval timelines, including draft-versus-template exception reporting and collaborative intake orchestration. Liz Colucci flagged interest in AI-powered Instareview that reduces contract analysis from weeks to seconds. ERP obligations integration and clause-level risk scoring are required, not optional.
• Three calls were recorded but only the October 8 demo is in completed state with 2395 seconds of audio. The remaining sessions were not recorded. Decision sequencing follows three steps - vendor research, business case, then summer/fall budget cycle - so current timeline may slip the 2025 window. The AI engagement is concrete enough to hold Hungry at the lower bound.
hard.primary_competitor: Status quo / no-decision (Stemline has no electronic contracting today)

north_star_verdict.headline: Constructive 8 Oct 2025 discovery that has stalled 9 months in Formal Evaluation with no pricing or business case; close pushed a third time to 28 Oct, and the 1 Jul re-engagement drew only an out-of-office reply.
north_star_verdict.math: Days to close: 114 (close 2026-10-28, today 2026-07-06). Time in Formal Evaluation: ~9.5 months (created 2025-09-23, stage never advanced). Close date pushed three times (2025-10-15 to 2026-04-23, 2026-02-17 to 2026-08-20, 2026-06-10 to 2026-10-28). Last genuine two-way buyer touch: neutral call with Liz Colucci on 2026-04-27 (~70 days ago); the project resurfaced at DPW New York, prompting Justin Ajmo's 2026-07-01 outreach, which returned only Liz's out-of-office auto-reply. No step-2 pricing or business-case activity has been logged since the 8 Oct 2025 call. Required pace: reactivate Erik Palkhiwala's budget cycle and a business case within 14-30 days to keep an Oct 2026 close credible.
evidence: Stage unchanged at Formal Evaluation for ~9.5 months (created 2025-09-23); no advance to Shortlisted or Vendor Selected.
Close date pushed three times (Apr to Aug to 28 Oct 2026) with no forward milestone attached to any push.
Last genuine buyer engagement was a neutral call with Liz Colucci on 2026-04-27; the 2026-07-01 reach-out returned only an out-of-office auto-reply.
Project resurfaced at DPW New York and Justin Ajmo re-engaged Liz on 2026-07-01 to get feedback (rep-side touch, awaiting buyer reply).
No step-2 pricing request or business-case assembly logged in the 9 months since the 8 Oct 2025 discovery call.
Budget explicitly 'TBD': Erik Palkhiwala said Stemline had already missed its 2025 summer/fall budgeting cycle (8 Oct 2025).
Two open items to the buyer since 8 Oct 2025 remain unconfirmed: all Merlin agents on Google Gemini, and biotech/pharma references.

Verdict trajectory: WEAKENING versus prior sweeps - a third close push and an OOO reply on the one re-engagement attempt; the single biggest blocker to close is buyer inertia plus an unconfirmed 2026 budget window.

metrics.narrative: Stemline's pain is articulated but never quantified. On the 8 Oct 2025 discovery call Liz Colucci said 'we don't have an electronic process. Everything we do right now is manual. So our entire contract process is manual, and as we're growing and getting bigger, the amount of contracts and the changes just have multiplied.' Erik Palkhiwala reinforced the driver: 'the volume of sourcing work is growing, but the headcount won't, so we have to find ways to leverage new and emerging workflow automation.' No numbers were given (contract volume, cycle time, review days saved, cost, or headcount avoided) on the call or in any Salesforce field, and no business case has been assembled. Quantifying contract cycle-time and volume with Liz and finance (Ugur Savan) is the near-term move that would let Stemline build the step-2 business case.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08 (Liz Colucci, Erik Palkhiwala)

economic_buyer.narrative: The budget authority is Erik Palkhiwala, who leads the Stemline US procurement function. On the 8 Oct 2025 call he described his own remit ('I lead the Stemline US procurement function... the center of gravity for the Menarini Group's global oncology business, so a lot, if not all, of the global oncology group's spend funnels through our organization') and owned the decision path, naming the exact three steps to a purchase. He also controls whether the Menarini parent gets pulled in ('anything that might make sense for us to take back to Menarini corporate'). He is identified (and named as an AI-requirements driver in AIS_Why__c) but engagement is passive: his last substantive appearance is the 8 Oct 2025 call, and the deal resurfacing at DPW New York has not converted into a fresh budget commitment. Re-securing a direct Erik conversation on the 2026 budget window is the gating move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and well-evidenced from the 8 Oct 2025 call, all CLM-centred. Erik Palkhiwala asked for 'specific AI-driven workflow automation capabilities that can shrink our end-to-end contract development, review and approval time frame,' naming intake and cross-functional review orchestration, legal-review support that compares 'a draft versus our template to rapidly identify whether any changes have been made and do exception reporting,' and 'components of ERP obligations integration.' A hard technical criterion is corporate LLM fit: 'we are a Google house, not a Microsoft house, our internal corporate-approved AI is Google Gemini,' with a requirement that Merlin agents run on Gemini rather than Azure/OpenAI. Further criteria: first-party ('usually our paper') contract templates, DocuSign e-signature, biotech/pharma domain fit, and indirect-services workflows ('the vast majority of what we buy are indirect services... not to buying goods'). Yana Levin added a phase-2 interest in the end-to-end sourcing module.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08 (Erik Palkhiwala, Yana Levin, Liz Colucci)

decision_process.narrative: Erik Palkhiwala laid out the buying process precisely on 8 Oct 2025: 'Step one is trying to understand and gather information to determine what the viable platform solutions are. Step two is going to be obtaining costs and assembling a business case. Step three will be seeking budget approval to establish a target implementation time frame and scope.' He flagged the forcing constraint himself: 'our annual budgeting cycle occurs during the summer and fall, so... we've missed this year's budgeting cycle,' meaning selection could not land in the 2025 window. The evaluation is a multi-function committee: Liz named clinical operations, an IT/AI representative, legal and finance (Ugur Savan) as demo attendees. Nine months on the deal is still in step one/two, which is why it reads as slowing rather than advancing.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08 (Erik Palkhiwala, Liz Colucci)

paper_process.narrative: The contracting shape is partly visible but no paper has started. Stemline uses first-party paper ('usually our paper... a majority percentage', Erik/Liz, 8 Oct 2025) and DocuSign for e-signature, with contract artefacts today scattered across a Google shared space for templates/clauses and SharePoint for the executed repository. Legal capacity exists on the account (Anna Arnopolsky, Assistant General Counsel R&D; Jessica Honan, Associate Director Corporate Counsel) and legal was named as a required demo attendee, but no MSA/SOW/redline activity has occurred because the deal has not reached commercials. The Menarini parent signatory relationship adds nuance: Stemline is 'signatories to some things, they're signatories to others' for oncology R&D. Visibility improves once Stemline funds the project and legal is engaged on a Zycus SaaS MSA plus order form.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08 (Erik Palkhiwala, Liz Colucci)

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is confirmed and owned by procurement/sourcing. Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing) stated the entire contract process is manual and that contract volume and change velocity have 'multiplied' as Stemline grows. Erik Palkhiwala framed the strategic version: headcount is flat while sourcing volume rises, forcing a move to workflow automation, and the buying profile is overwhelmingly indirect services rather than goods, which makes CLM fit for services procurement the core need. The pain owner is Liz, shared across a committee spanning clinical operations, IT/AI, legal, finance and quality. The confirmed gap is that the pain has not been converted into quantified impact, so it does not yet carry a funded business case.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08 (Liz Colucci, Erik Palkhiwala)

champion.narrative: Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing; SF primary Evaluator contact) is the developing champion. She sourced Zycus ('I asked people for recommendations... and I did some internet searches'), shaped the demo agenda toward CLM, named the internal committee, and set up demo scheduling, and she has direct working access to the economic buyer Erik Palkhiwala (both on the discovery call). What is still missing to call her strong: she has not proactively driven the evaluation forward in the 9 months since, has not requested pricing or moved to a business case, and her most recent touch is an out-of-office auto-reply to Justin Ajmo's 2026-07-01 outreach. She is a genuine internal advocate but currently passive and single-threaded; converting her DPW New York interest into a scheduled working session is what would upgrade her from developing to active.

competition.narrative: Salesforce logs Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis and Ironclad as competitors, but Stemline deliberately withheld its shortlist: when asked, Erik Palkhiwala said 'we would be violating confidentiality' (8 Oct 2025), so there is no evidence of which rival leads or how Zycus compares head-to-head. The genuinely dominant threat on this deal is not a vendor but inertia: a fully manual buyer that admitted it missed its budget cycle and has pushed close three times can easily default to no-decision. The wedge to win is domain and AI fit: Merlin's contract-review-in-seconds and Instareview against Stemline's manual pain, Gemini-LLM flexibility for a Google house, and biotech/pharma references for an indirect-services CLM use case. Both AI/reference proof points remain unconfirmed since 8 Oct 2025, so the differentiation is claimed but not yet landed.
sources: SF Competitors__c (Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, Ironclad)

deal_movement.summary: Trajectory is weakening. After a strong 8 Oct 2025 discovery, the deal never left Formal Evaluation: amount was set to $130k on 2025-10-15 and close date has slipped three times (Apr, then Aug, then 28 Oct 2026). A neutral call with Liz on 2026-04-27 and a DPW New York encounter kept a pulse, but the 2026-07-01 re-engagement drew only an out-of-office reply, and Division was re-segmented New Markets to Mid-Enterprise the same day. No pricing or business-case step has been logged since discovery.

items.change: Close date pushed 23 Apr to 20 Aug 2026
Neutral outbound call with Liz Colucci (Step #4 follow-up)
Close date pushed 20 Aug to 28 Oct 2026 after project resurfaced at DPW New York
Justin Ajmo re-engaged Liz for feedback (returned out-of-office); Division re-segmented to Mid-Enterprise

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is status-quo / no-decision, not a named rival: Stemline has no electronic contracting today, admitted it missed its 2025 budget cycle, and has pushed close three times. The four logged vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis, Ironclad) are unranked because Erik Palkhiwala declined to name the shortlist on 8 Oct 2025 ('we would be violating confidentiality'); iCertis and Ironclad are the more relevant CLM-specific peers given the iContract-first scope, while Coupa and SAP Ariba are broader suite alternatives. No 2026 call evidence has re-ranked any of them, so threat levels are held as carried forward. We win on AI/domain fit, not by out-featuring a known finalist.

competitors.name: Status quo / no-decision (manual contracting)
competitors.quote: we don't have an electronic process. Everything we do right now is manual.
competitors.how_we_win: Convert manual pain into a quantified cycle-time/volume business case with Liz and Ugur Savan so Stemline can fund the project in its next budget cycle.

competitors.name: iCertis
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast Merlin contract-review-in-seconds and Instareview plus first-party-paper template automation for indirect services; land the biotech/pharma references.

competitors.name: Ironclad
competitors.how_we_win: Position the full source-to-pay platform behind the CLM entry point (sourcing module Yana asked to see) so Stemline buys a growth path, not point CLM.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with single-code-base Merlin AI and Gemini-LLM flexibility for a Google house; keep the CLM-first, indirect-services scope Stemline actually asked for.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Position mid-market fit and speed-to-value versus a heavier suite; anchor on the manual CLM pain Ariba over-serves for a company this size.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on CLM automation, AI and indirect-services fit; exposed on the two proof points we owe (Gemini-agent confirmation and biotech references) and only partly proven on ERP integration.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a stalled early-stage evaluation where inertia, not a rival, is the threat.
flags: Break the no-decision risk: co-build a quantified indirect-services CLM business case with Liz and finance (Ugur Savan) so Stemline can enter its next budget cycle with numbers.
Multi-thread beyond Liz and Erik into IT/AI (Vinita Raina) and legal (Anna Arnopolsky) so one out-of-office champion cannot stall the evaluation again.
Land the two open proof points that differentiate us where the buyer won't name rivals: all-Merlin-agents-on-Gemini confirmation and biotech/pharma indirect-services references.

champion_strength.summary: Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing) is a real but developing champion: she sourced Zycus, shaped the CLM-first demo, named the committee and holds direct access to economic buyer Erik Palkhiwala. What is missing to call her strong: she has not driven the evaluation forward in 9 months, has not requested pricing or moved to a business case, and her latest touch is an out-of-office reply to the 2026-07-01 outreach. She is single-threaded and currently passive.
champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 2025-10-08; SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator, primary); SF Next_Step__c 2026-07-01

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The AI story resonated - Erik Palkhiwala proactively raised Gemini-LLM architecture and Liz flagged interest in AI-powered Instareview - but Zycus is under-positioned because the pivotal question (do ALL Merlin agents run on Gemini) was left as an open action item on 8 Oct 2025 and never closed. Putting the Merlin/Gemini answer and an AI-for-CLM narrative in front of IT/AI (Vinita Raina) is the move.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c is 7 (Moderate), but the 8 Oct 2025 call shows genuine buyer-driven AI demand: Erik Palkhiwala raised corporate-LLM architecture unprompted and Liz Colucci flagged AI-powered Instareview to cut contract analysis 'from weeks to seconds', suggesting the field slightly under-rates real AI appetite. The unresolved Gemini-agent confirmation is the risk to converting that appetite.

items.detail: No confirmed budget; Stemline missed its 2025 summer/fall cycle and the 2026 window is unconfirmed.
Manual, no-system buyer prone to no-decision inertia; a defer-indefinitely outcome is the biggest single threat.
Close date pushed three times (Apr to Aug to 28 Oct 2026); 9.5 months in Formal Evaluation with no stage advance.
Economic buyer Erik Palkhiwala passive since discovery; no direct re-engagement in ~9 months.
Biotech/pharma reference customers requested on 8 Oct 2025 still not delivered.
Whether all Merlin agents run on Stemline's Google Gemini instance remains unconfirmed since 8 Oct 2025.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cooling: one rich discovery call 9 months ago, a neutral April call, a DPW New York mention, and an out-of-office reply to the July re-engagement. The deal is alive but thinning and single-threaded.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: An early-stage CLM evaluation that started strong on the 8 Oct 2025 discovery has stalled 9 months with no pricing and no business case, and has slipped close three times to 28 Oct; the one thing between us and progress is reactivating Erik Palkhiwala's budget cycle, not beating a named rival.

win_position.text: We are a credible early contender: Liz Colucci (Director, Vendor & Sourcing) sourced us and shaped a CLM-first demo, and US procurement head Erik Palkhiwala engaged deeply on AI and Gemini-LLM fit on the 8 Oct 2025 call.
No buyer preference is established: Erik declined to name the shortlist ('we would be violating confidentiality', 8 Oct 2025), so Coupa, SAP Ariba, iCertis and Ironclad all sit in the field unranked.
The real threat is inertia, not a rival: Stemline has no electronic contracting today, admitted it missed its 2025 budget cycle, and has pushed close three times (Apr to Aug to 28 Oct 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is flat: the opportunity has sat in Formal Evaluation for ~9 months since 23 Sep 2025 with no stage advance and no pricing or business-case step logged.
The last genuine buyer touch was a neutral call with Liz on 27 Apr 2026; the 1 Jul reach-out drew only an out-of-office auto-reply.
One live thread remains: the project resurfaced at DPW New York and Justin Ajmo re-engaged Liz on 1 Jul 2026 to get feedback.

customer_commitment.text: No commercial commitment exists: no pricing requested, no business case assembled, and Erik put budget approval as 'TBD' after missing the 2025 cycle (8 Oct 2025).
Committee breadth is real: Liz named clinical operations, IT/AI, legal and finance (Ugur Savan) as demo stakeholders, signalling an organised buying group.
Two commitments to the buyer, confirming all Merlin agents run on Google Gemini and sharing biotech/pharma references, remain unconfirmed since 8 Oct 2025.

deal_risk.text: No-decision is the primary risk: a manual-process buyer that missed its budget window can defer indefinitely rather than choose a vendor.
Single-threaded to Liz Colucci and Erik Palkhiwala; IT/AI (Vinita Raina), finance (Ugur Savan) and legal (Anna Arnopolsky) are mapped but never engaged directly.

deal_momentum.evidence: ~9.5 months in Formal Evaluation, no stage move, close pushed three times, last buyer call 27 Apr 2026, July outreach hit OOO.

customer_commitment.evidence: No pricing, no business case, budget 'TBD'; Stemline missed its 2025 budgeting cycle.

deal_risk.evidence: No-decision/inertia risk on a manual-process buyer; economic buyer passive ~9 months; single-threaded.

scope_change.from: iContract-first CLM with Merlin and iCompliance
scope_change.to: iContract-first CLM with Merlin and iCompliance
scope_change.detail: Scope has not narrowed; amount rose $100k to $130k (2025-10-15) and the buyer expressed interest in later add-ons (PO/invoice management, end-to-end sourcing module). The 2026-07-01 Division change (New Markets to Mid-Enterprise) is internal segmentation, not a scope cut.

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer neutral and still in the research phase; Erik declined to name the shortlist and gave no Zycus-specific lean. Constructive Zycus-specific questions on 8 Oct 2025 have not recurred, so the earlier medium interest has decayed.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only (manual process, contract volume rising, flat headcount, indirect-services focus); no cost, cycle-time or savings figure quantified and step-2 business case not yet assembled.

items.action: Reach Erik Palkhiwala directly to confirm the revived DPW New York project and 2026 budget window.
items.trigger: 1 Jul outreach to Liz returned an out-of-office auto-reply; project flagged alive at DPW New York.
items.expected_effect: Reactivates the economic buyer and confirms whether the evaluation is genuinely restarting, breaking the no-decision risk.

items.action: Close the open Gemini question: confirm all Merlin agents run on Stemline's Google Gemini instance.
items.trigger: Zycus left the all-agents-on-Gemini answer as an unresolved action item.
items.expected_effect: Removes the pivotal AI technical objection and re-earns credibility with the IT/AI evaluator.

items.action: Send Liz Colucci biotech/pharma references and a phase-2 add-on roadmap for indirect-services CLM.
items.trigger: Yana and Erik asked for sector and category-mix references still not delivered.
items.expected_effect: Satisfies the domain-validation criterion and gives the champion internal ammunition to re-open the evaluation.

items.action: Book a working session with Vinita Raina to validate Gemini-LLM integration for the Merlin agents.
items.trigger: IT/AI owner of the LLM criterion has never been engaged directly.
items.expected_effect: Adds a second thread and de-risks the technical gate before commercials.

items.action: Co-build a quantified indirect-services CLM business case with Liz and finance lead Ugur Savan.
items.trigger: Buyer's step 2 is 'obtaining costs and assembling a business case'; none exists after 9 months.
items.expected_effect: Seeds the numbers Stemline needs to enter its next budget cycle, converting pain into funding.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 30 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 52.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 15 is anchored on the stage win (30), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
